questions.question: In vue-i18n, how are pluralization forms separated in a message string?
options: Using commas: "No items, One item, {count} items"
Using pipe characters: "No items | One item | {count} items"
Using numbered keys: "0:No items|1:One item|n:{count} items"
Using array syntax in the JSON file

questions.question: What does changing the locale ref from useI18n() accomplish in a Vue app?
options: It reloads the page with the new language from the server
It only updates the current component's translations
It reactively switches all translations across the entire app
It stores the preference in localStorage for future visits

questions.question: What routing strategy does @nuxtjs/i18n use with 'prefix_except_default' config?
options: All routes have a locale prefix including the default language
No routes have a locale prefix — the Accept-Language header is used
Non-default locales get a prefix (e.g., /es/about) while default has no prefix
Routes use query parameters like ?lang=es instead of path prefixes
